{{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house =[[Imperial House of Japan]] |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler =[[Mononobe no Moriya]] |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a [[Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of [[Buddhism]], partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of [[conservatism]] and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Shrine]] by [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], the daughter of [[Emperor Suinin]]. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the [[Emperor Yōmei]] died after a very short reign. [[Mononobe no Moriya]], the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on [[Mononobe no Moriya]] for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Shrine]], a [[Shinto shrine]] which doubled as an imperial [[Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)

Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)
==Notes== <references group="notes"/>
==References== *Sansom, George (1958). ''A History of Japan to 1334''. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

[[sn:Mononobe clan]] [[en:Mononobe clan]] [[sn:ononobe clan]]

{{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house =[[Imperial House of Japan]] |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler =[[Mononobe no Moriya]] |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a [[Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of [[Buddhism]], partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of [[conservatism]] and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Shrine]] by [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], the daughter of [[Emperor Suinin]]. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the [[Emperor Yōmei]] died after a very short reign. [[Mononobe no Moriya]], the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on [[Mononobe no Moriya]] for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Shrine]], a [[Shinto shrine]] which doubled as an imperial [[Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)

Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)
==Notes== <references group="notes"/>

[[sn:Mononobe clan]] [[en:Mononobe clan]] [[sn:ononobe clan]]

{{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house =[[Imperial House of Japan]] |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler =[[Mononobe no Moriya]] |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a [[Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of [[Buddhism]], partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of [[conservatism]] and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], the daughter of [[Emperor Suinin]]. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the [[Emperor Yōmei]] died after a very short reign. [[Mononobe no Moriya]], the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on [[Mononobe no Moriya]] for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a [[Shinto shrine]] which doubled as an imperial [[Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)

'''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)
==Notes== <references group="notes"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house =[[Imperial House of Japan]] |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler =[[Mononobe no Moriya]] |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a [[Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of [[Buddhism]], partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of [[conservatism]] and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], the daughter of [[Emperor Suinin]]. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the [[Emperor Yōmei]] died after a very short reign. [[Mononobe no Moriya]], the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on [[Mononobe no Moriya]] for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a [[Shinto shrine]] which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)
==Notes== <references group="notes"/>
==References== *Sansom, George (1958). ''A History of Japan to 1334''. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.
==See also== *[[Mononobe no Arakabi]] *[[Mononobe no Moriya]] *[[Mononobe no Okoshi]] *[[Isonokami no Maro]] *''[[Kujiki]]'' *[[Mononobe-jinja]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house =[[Imperial House of Japan]] |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler =[[Mononobe no Moriya]] |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a [[Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of [[conservatism]] and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], the daughter of [[Emperor Suinin]]. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the [[Emperor Yōmei]] died after a very short reign. [[Mononobe no Moriya]], the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on [[Mononobe no Moriya]] for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a [[Shinto shrine]] which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)
==Notes== <references group="notes"/>
==References== *Sansom, George (1958). ''A History of Japan to 1334''. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

{{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house =[[Imperial House of Japan]] |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler =[[Mononobe no Moriya]] |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a [[Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of [[:en:Conservatism|Conservatism]] and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] died after a very short reign. [[Mononobe no Moriya]], the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on [[Mononobe no Moriya]] for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a [[Shinto shrine]] which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)

==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)

{{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house =[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler =[[Mononobe no Moriya]] |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of [[:en:Conservatism|Conservatism]] and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] died after a very short reign. [[Mononobe no Moriya]], the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on [[Mononobe no Moriya]] for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a [[Shinto shrine]] which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)

==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)

revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house =[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler =[[Mononobe no Moriya]] |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of [[:en:Conservatism|Conservatism]] and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by [[Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] died after a very short reign. [[Mononobe no Moriya]], the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on [[Mononobe no Moriya]] for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a [[Shinto shrine]] which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)
==Notes== <references group="notes"/>
==References== *Sansom, George (1958). ''A History of Japan to 1334''. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.
==See also== *[[:en:Mononobe no Arakabi|Mononobe no Arakabi]] *[[Mononobe no Moriya]] *[[Mononobe no Okoshi]] *[[Isonokami no Maro]] *''[[Kujiki]]'' *[[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]]

==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)

revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house =[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler =[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of [[:en:Conservatism|Conservatism]] and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] died after a very short reign. [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a [[Shinto shrine]] which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)
==Notes== <references group="notes"/>
==References== *Sansom, George (1958). ''A History of Japan to 1334''. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.
==See also== *[[:en:Mononobe no Arakabi|Mononobe no Arakabi]] *[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] *[[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Mononobe no Okoshi]] *[[Isonokami no Maro]] *''[[Kujiki]]'' *[[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]]

[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)

[[Category:Pages without translation templates]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[de:Mononobe (Klan)]] [[es:Clan Mononobe]] [[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]] [[fr:Clan Mononobe]] [[id:Klan Mononobe]] [[it:Clan Mononobe]] [[ja:物部氏]] [[ko:모노노베씨]] [[pt:Clã Mononobe]] [[ru:Мононобэ (род)]] [[sh:Mononobe]] [[uk:Рід Мононобе]] [[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]] [[zh:物部氏]] [[zh-classical:物部氏]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1755836]] Wikidata name: Mononobe clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house =[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler =[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of [[:en:Conservatism|Conservatism]] and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] died after a very short reign. [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)

The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] died after a very short reign. [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh-classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1755836]] Wikidata name: Mononobe clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house =[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler =[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of [[:en:Conservatism|Conservatism]] and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] died after a very short reign. [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)
==Notes== <references group="notes"/>
==References== *Sansom, George (1958). ''A History of Japan to 1334''. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.
==See also== *[[:en:Mononobe no Arakabi|Mononobe no Arakabi]] *[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] *[[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Mononobe no Okoshi]] *[[Isonokami no Maro]] *''[[Kujiki]]'' *[[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]]

Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh-classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1755836]] Wikidata name: Mononobe clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house =[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler =[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of [[:en:Conservatism|Conservatism]] and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] died after a very short reign. [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh-classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1755836]] Wikidata name: Mononobe clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house =[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler =[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of [[:en:Conservatism|Conservatism]] and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] died after a very short reign. [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)
==Notes== <references group="notes"/>
==References== *Sansom, George (1958). ''A History of Japan to 1334''. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.
==See also== *[[:en:Mononobe no Arakabi|Mononobe no Arakabi]] *[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] *[[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Mononobe no Okoshi]] *[[Isonokami no Maro]] *''[[Kujiki]]'' *[[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]]

==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh-classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1755836]] Wikidata name: Mononobe clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house =[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler =[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of [[:en:Conservatism|Conservatism]] and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] died after a very short reign. [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)
