<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh-classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1755836]] Wikidata name: Mononobe clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house =[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler =[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of [[:en:Conservatism|Conservatism]] and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] died after a very short reign. [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)
==Notes== <references group="notes"/>
==References== *Sansom, George (1958). ''A History of Japan to 1334''. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.
==See also== *[[:en:Mononobe no Arakabi|Mononobe no Arakabi]] *[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] *[[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Mononobe no Okoshi]] *[[Isonokami no Maro]] *''[[Kujiki]]'' *[[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]]

==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh_classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh_classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh-classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1755836]] Wikidata name: Mononobe clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house =[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler =[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of [[:en:Conservatism|Conservatism]] and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] died after a very short reign. [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of [[:en:Conservatism|Conservatism]] and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh_classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh_classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh-classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1755836]] Wikidata name: Mononobe clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house =[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler =[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of [[:en:Conservatism|Conservatism]] and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] died after a very short reign. [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)
==Notes== <references group="notes"/>
==References== *Sansom, George (1958). ''A History of Japan to 1334''. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh_classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh_classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh-classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1755836]] Wikidata name: Mononobe clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house =[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler =[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of [[:en:Conservatism|Conservatism]] and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] died after a very short reign. [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)

He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] died after a very short reign. [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Arakabi|Arakabi]]''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh_classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh_classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh-classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1755836]] Wikidata name: Mononobe clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house =[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler =[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of [[:en:Conservatism|Conservatism]] and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] died after a very short reign. [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]], the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''[[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Okoshi]]''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''[[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Moriya]]''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)

The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japanese}} aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}}, partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of {{ill|Conservatism|en|Conservatism|WD=Q7169|lt=Conservatism}} and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei}} died after a very short reign. {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}}, the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}} for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''{{ill|Mononobe no Okoshi|en|Mononobe no Okoshi|WD=Q11570693|lt=Okoshi}}''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''{{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Moriya}}''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''{{ill|Mononobe no Arakabi|en|Mononobe no Arakabi|WD=Q1114837|lt=Arakabi}}''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)
==Notes== <references group="notes"/>
==References== *Sansom, George (1958). ''A History of Japan to 1334''. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh_classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh_classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh-classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1755836]] Wikidata name: Mononobe clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house ={{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler ={{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}} |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japanese}} aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}}, partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of {{ill|Conservatism|en|Conservatism|WD=Q7169|lt=Conservatism}} and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei}} died after a very short reign. {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}}, the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}} for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''{{ill|Mononobe no Okoshi|en|Mononobe no Okoshi|WD=Q11570693|lt=Okoshi}}''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''{{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Moriya}}''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''{{ill|Mononobe no Arakabi|en|Mononobe no Arakabi|WD=Q1114837|lt=Arakabi}}''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)
==Notes== <references group="notes"/>
==References== *Sansom, George (1958). ''A History of Japan to 1334''. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}} for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''{{ill|Mononobe no Okoshi|en|Mononobe no Okoshi|WD=Q11570693|lt=Okoshi}}''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''{{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Moriya}}''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''{{ill|Mononobe no Arakabi|en|Mononobe no Arakabi|WD=Q1114837|lt=Arakabi}}''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh_classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh_classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh-classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1755836]] Wikidata name: Mononobe clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house ={{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler ={{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}} |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japanese}} aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}}, partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of {{ill|Conservatism|en|Conservatism|WD=Q7169|lt=Conservatism}} and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei}} died after a very short reign. {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}}, the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}} for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].

Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}} for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>

==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''{{ill|Mononobe no Okoshi|en|Mononobe no Okoshi|WD=Q11570693|lt=Okoshi}}''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''{{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Moriya}}''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''{{ill|Mononobe no Arakabi|en|Mononobe no Arakabi|WD=Q1114837|lt=Arakabi}}''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)
==Notes== <references group="notes"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh-classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh-classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh-classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]]
{{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house ={{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler ={{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}} |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japanese}} aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}}, partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of {{ill|Conservatism|en|Conservatism|WD=Q7169|lt=Conservatism}} and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei}} died after a very short reign. {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}}, the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}} for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''{{ill|Mononobe no Okoshi|en|Mononobe no Okoshi|WD=Q11570693|lt=Okoshi}}''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''{{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Moriya}}''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''{{ill|Mononobe no Arakabi|en|Mononobe no Arakabi|WD=Q1114837|lt=Arakabi}}''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)

Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}} for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''{{ill|Mononobe no Okoshi|en|Mononobe no Okoshi|WD=Q11570693|lt=Okoshi}}''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''{{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Moriya}}''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''{{ill|Mononobe no Arakabi|en|Mononobe no Arakabi|WD=Q1114837|lt=Arakabi}}''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)
==Notes== <references group="notes"/>
==References== *Sansom, George (1958). ''A History of Japan to 1334''. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.
==See also== *{{ill|Mononobe no Arakabi|en|Mononobe no Arakabi|WD=Q1114837|lt=Mononobe no Arakabi}} *{{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}} *{{ill|Mononobe no Okoshi|en|Mononobe no Okoshi|WD=Q11570693|lt=Mononobe no Okoshi}} *[[Isonokami no Maro]] *''[[Kujiki]]'' *{{ill|Mononobe Shrine|en|Mononobe Shrine|WD=Q11570699|lt=Mononobe Shrine}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh-classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh-classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh-classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]]
{{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house ={{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler ={{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}} |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japanese}} aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}}, partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of {{ill|Conservatism|en|Conservatism|WD=Q7169|lt=Conservatism}} and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei}} died after a very short reign. {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}}, the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}} for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''{{ill|Mononobe no Okoshi|en|Mononobe no Okoshi|WD=Q11570693|lt=Okoshi}}''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''{{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Moriya}}''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''{{ill|Mononobe no Arakabi|en|Mononobe no Arakabi|WD=Q1114837|lt=Arakabi}}''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)
==Notes== <references group="notes"/>
==References== *Sansom, George (1958). ''A History of Japan to 1334''. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.
==See also== *{{ill|Mononobe no Arakabi|en|Mononobe no Arakabi|WD=Q1114837|lt=Mononobe no Arakabi}} *{{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}} *{{ill|Mononobe no Okoshi|en|Mononobe no Okoshi|WD=Q11570693|lt=Mononobe no Okoshi}} *[[Isonokami no Maro]] *''[[Kujiki]]'' *{{ill|Mononobe Shrine|en|Mononobe Shrine|WD=Q11570699|lt=Mononobe Shrine}}

Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|1=Mononobe no Moriya}} for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>

==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''{{ill|Mononobe no Okoshi|en|Mononobe no Okoshi|WD=Q11570693|lt=Okoshi|1=Mononobe no Okoshi}}''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''{{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Moriya|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''{{ill|Mononobe no Arakabi|en|Mononobe no Arakabi|WD=Q1114837|lt=Arakabi|1=Mononobe no Arakabi}}''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)
==Notes== <references group="notes"/>
==References== *Sansom, George (1958). ''A History of Japan to 1334''. Stanford, California: Stanford University Press.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh-classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh-classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Mononobe (Klan)]][[en:Mononobe clan]][[es:Clan Mononobe]][[fa:خاندان مونونوبه]][[fr:Clan Mononobe]][[id:Klan Mononobe]][[it:Clan Mononobe]][[ja:物部氏]][[ko:모노노베씨]][[pt:Clã Mononobe]][[ru:Мононобэ (род)]][[sh:Mononobe]][[uk:Рід Мононобе]][[vi:Gia tộc Mononobe]][[zh-classical:物部氏]][[zh:物部氏]]
{{Short description|Japanese aristocratic kin group}} {{family name hatnote|Mononobe|lang=Japanese}} {{refimprove|date=March 2022}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname nihongo = 物部氏 |parent house ={{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}} |titles =Various |founder =Mononobe no Toochone |final ruler ={{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|1=Mononobe no Moriya}} |dissolution =587 |ruled until =587, Battle at Mount Shigi |cadet branches =[[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]] }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mononobe clan'''|物部氏|Mononobe uji}} was a {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japanese|1=Japan}} aristocratic kin group [[Uji (clan)|(''uji'')]] of the [[Kofun period]], known for its military opposition to the [[Soga clan]]. The Mononobe were opposed to the spread of {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}}, partly on religious grounds, claiming that the local deities would be offended by the worshiping of foreign deities, but also as the result of feelings of {{ill|Conservatism|en|Conservatism|WD=Q7169|lt=Conservatism|1=Conservatism}} and a degree of xenophobia. The [[Nakatomi clan]], ancestors of the [[Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara]], were also Shinto ritualists allied with the Mononobe in opposition to Buddhism.
The Mononobe, like many other major families of the time, were something of a corporation or guild in addition to being a proper family by blood-relation. While the only members of the clan to appear in any significant way in the historical record were statesmen, the clan as a whole was known as the Corporation of Arms or Armorers.
==History== {{Further|Soga–Mononobe conflict}} The Mononobe were said to have been descended from [[List of Japanese deities#Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], (饒速日命), a legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. His descendant Mononobe no Toochine (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan, was given [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}. He then began using the name Mononobe.
In the 6th century, a number of violent clashes erupted between the Mononobe and the Soga clan. According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', one particularly important conflict occurred after the {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei|1=Emperor Yōmei}} died after a very short reign. {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}, the head of the clan, supported one prince to succeed Yōmei, while [[Soga no Umako]] chose another. The conflict came to a head in a battle at Kisuri (present-day Osaka) in the year 587, where the Mononobe clan were defeated and crushed at the [[:en:Battle of Shigisan|Battle of Shigisan]]. Following Moriya's death, Buddhism saw a further spread in Japan.<ref group="notes">Read more in the article on {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|1=Mononobe no Moriya}} for recent findings on a possible sponsorship for Buddhism by the Mononobe.</ref>
In 686, the Mononobe reformed as the [[Mononobe clan|Isonokami clan]], named thus due to their close ties with [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]], a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} which doubled as an imperial [[:en:Armory (military)|armory]].
==Family Tree== '''[[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-mikoto]]''' (饒速日命), legendary figure who is said to have ruled Yamato before the conquest of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. ┃ '''Umashimaji-no-mikoto''' (可美真手命) ┇ (5 generations missing) ┇ '''Mononobe no Tōchine''' (物部十千根), known as the founder of the clan. ┃ '''Mononobe no Ikui''' (物部胆咋) ┃ '''Ikoto''' (物部五十琴) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Ikofutsu''' (物部伊莒弗) '''Mukiri''' (麦入) '''Iwamochi''' (石持) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ ┃ '''Me''' (目) '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) '''Makura''' (真椋) '''Oomae''' (大前) '''Omae''' (小前) '''Ushiro''' (菟代) ┃ '''Arayama''' (荒山) ┃ '''{{ill|Mononobe no Okoshi|en|Mononobe no Okoshi|WD=Q11570693|lt=Okoshi|1=Mononobe no Okoshi}}''' (尾輿) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Mikari''' (御狩) '''{{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Moriya|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}''' (守屋) '''Nieko''' (贄子), his daughter married [[Soga no Umako]] ┃ '''Me''' (目) ┃ '''Umaro''' (宇麻呂) ┃ '''[[Isonokami no Maro]]''' (石上麻呂), changed his surname and founded the Isonokami clan (石上氏)
Descendants of Mononobe no Futsukuru (物部布都久留), see above tree. '''Futsukuru''' (布都久留) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Itabi''' (木蓮子) '''Ogoto''' (小事) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Masara''' (麻佐良) '''Yakahime''' (宅媛), consort of [[Emperor Ankan]] ┃ '''{{ill|Mononobe no Arakabi|en|Mononobe no Arakabi|WD=Q1114837|lt=Arakabi|1=Mononobe no Arakabi}}''' (麁鹿火) ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ '''Iwayumi''' (石弓) '''Kagehime''' (影媛)
